The author is dealt two cards and needs to determine the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards dealt. This can be calculated using combinatorial functions. The total number of possible 5-card hands is $ \binom{52}{5} $. The number of hands that contain at least one heart can be found by subtracting the number of hands with no hearts from the total. The number of hands with no hearts is $ \binom{48}{5} $. Therefore, the probability is:

$$
P(\text{at least one heart}) = 1 - \frac{\binom{48}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}}
$$

This expression represents the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards, expressed in terms of combinatorial functions.
